paper_title: Chemical and Taxonomic Analysis of Sinking Particles

methods.description: Process for analyzing the chemical composition of sinking particles collected by sediment traps, including biogenic silica, organic and inorganic carbon, and lithogenic material.

steps.description: Sinking particles collected by the sediment traps were sieved through a 1-mm mesh to remove large swimmers.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve particles to remove large swimmers

steps.description: The fine particle fraction (less than 1 mm) was equally split into appropriate aliquots for each analysis using a rotary splitter.
steps.searchable_summary: split particles into aliquots using rotary splitter

steps.description: Biogenic silica of sinking particles was measured by a modified alkaline leaching method of Mortlock and Froelich (1989).
steps.searchable_summary: measure biogenic silica using alkaline leaching

steps.description: Particulate organic and total carbon and nitrogen in the sinking particles were measured by a CHN Analyzer (NCS2500, Thermo Ques).
steps.searchable_summary: measure organic and total carbon and nitrogen using CHN Analyzer

steps.description: Organic matter (OM) content was estimated by 'Redfield ratio' (Redfield et al. 1963) and organic carbon content as described in Honda et al. (2002).
steps.searchable_summary: estimate organic matter content using Redfield ratio

steps.description: Inorganic carbon content was estimated by the difference of total carbon and organic carbon contents.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate inorganic carbon content by difference

steps.description: CaCO3 was calculated by multiplying inorganic carbon content by 100/12.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate CaCO3 from inorganic carbon content

steps.description: The lithogenic material (LM) concentration was estimated using the following formula.

methods.description: Procedure for preparing sediment trap sample slides and counting radiolarian skeletons to calculate radiolarian flux.

steps.description: Sediment trap sample slides for light microscope observation were prepared using the methods described by.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sample slides for microscope observation

steps.description: We counted all radiolarian skeletons from the 45 μm-1 mm fraction encountered on each slide with an Olympus BX43 transmitted light microscope at 200x or 400x magnification.
The radiolarian flux (no. of specimens m À2 d À1) was calculated from count data using the following formula.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate radiolarian flux from count data

steps.description: All skeletal fragments that could be allocated to a species, or a group of species, were included in our counts.
steps.searchable_summary: include identifiable skeletal fragments in counts

steps.description: Since skeletal fragments that cannot be identified at the species level were also counted as Spumellarida indet. or Nassellarida indet., the number of skeletal fragments that could not be counted is estimated to be negligible in 45 μm-1 mm fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: count unidentifiable skeletal fragments as indeterminate

steps.description: Radiolarians from the < 45 μm fraction were not included in this study.
We defined adult and juvenile forms of Actinommidae, Amphimelissa setosa, and Pseudodictyophimus according to as follows: the two-shelled forms of Actinommidae were defined as juvenile.
steps.searchable_summary: define adult and juvenile forms of radiolarians
